item.title: Exclusive: Two Sigma, D.E. Shaw join Wall Street push against US SEC's bid to relax quarterly reporting
item.description: Two Sigma Investments and D.E. Shaw are among several Wall Street investment firms and hedge funds that are fighting a proposal to allow companies to opt out of ​quarterly reporting, according to people familiar with the matter, on concerns it would reduce the flow of key financial information for investors in public companies.

item.title: Cadence, Nvidia working together on developing AI for robotics
item.description: Cadence Design Systems ​and Nvidia are partnering to further ‌the development of artificial intelligence for robots, the CEOs of the two companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Europe sees record jet inflows from US in April
item.description: Europe was seeing record inflows of jet fuel from the United States, data from Kpler and LSEG showed, as the region attempts to shore up fuel supplies to make up for the disruption ​of imports from the Middle East.

item.title: Wall Street still betting on 2026 deal boom, but Middle East unrest adds caution
item.description: Wall Street's biggest banks still expect 2026 to be a strong year for dealmaking, although turmoil in the Middle East ​that has roiled global markets has tempered executive optimism.

item.title: L3Harris announces $1.27 billion expansion project at Virginia site
item.description: L3Harris Technologies and Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger said on Wednesday the defense supplier will ​invest $1.27 billion to further the solid rocket ‌motor production capacity at its Orange County site.

item.title: Tax enforcement weakened after Trump job cuts, IRS data shows
item.description: The Trump administration dramatically reduced efforts to pursue tax cheats in 2025, ​government data shows, shedding tens of thousands of employees at the Internal Revenue Service, including those charged with ‌enforcement.

item.title: Robinhood, Webull jump after US SEC approves removal of day-trading limit for smaller investors
item.description: Shares of retail trading platforms Robinhood and Webull surged on Wednesday after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission paved the way for a new regulatory ​framework on day-trading limit for smaller investors.

item.title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
item.description: European Central Bank supervisors are set to warn bankers ​about the risks posed by ‌Anthropic's new artificial intelligence model that might supercharge cyberattacks, one source familiar ​with the situation told Reuters.

item.title: Allbirds shares jump over 400% on plans to pivot to AI from sneakers
item.description: Shares of Allbirds surged more than five-fold on Wednesday after the footwear maker said it was raising capital and pivoting towards AI computing infrastructure.

item.title: Big ad agencies settle US FTC probe into alleged boycott over political content
item.description: Three large advertising agencies ​settled a U.S. Federal Trade Commission probe accusing them of violating ‌antitrust law by conspiring to boycott online media platforms based on political content they didn't like, the agency said on Wednesday.

item.title: Middle East war damage to energy assets may cost up to $58 billion, research firm Rystad says
item.description: The Middle East conflict could saddle the region with as much as $58 billion in repair costs for energy-linked infrastructure, with oil ​and gas facilities alone accounting for up to $50 billion, according to ‌a report by Rystad Energy.

item.title: BYD shuns price war in South Africa to build EV brand
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD ‌is deliberately avoiding a price war in South Africa as competition ramps up in the continent's second biggest auto market, its local managing director told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: EU warns Meta WhatsApp AI fee breaches antitrust rules, orders rollback
item.description: The European Commission said on Wednesday it intended to order Meta Platforms to reinstate rival artificial intelligence assistants on its WhatsApp messaging service after the U.S. ​tech giant imposed an access fee.

item.title: Next head of UK's PRA says AI threats and opportunities should be addressed equally
item.description: The incoming head of the UK's prudential ​regulator for banks and ‌insurers said on Wednesday risks and opportunities arising from ​the widespread adoption ​of artificial intelligence in the ⁠financial sector should ​be addressed equally.

item.title: US can't wait forever to bring down fiscal deficits, IMF says
item.description: The United States needs to reduce its fiscal deficit ​by about four percentage points of ‌GDP from the current level of 7% and needs a robust, credible plan ​to do so, International Monetary ​Fund Fiscal Affairs Director Rodrigo Valdes ⁠said on Wednesday.

item.title: US home builder sentiment drops to seven-month low in April, NAHB survey says
item.description: U.S. homebuilder sentiment deteriorated in April, hitting a seven-month low as the war with Iran led to higher prices for materials and mortgage rates as well as increased economic uncertainty, a ​survey showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Pizza chains Papa John's and Pizza Hut edge closer to finding new owners, sources say
item.description: Two of America's most popular national pizza chains are edging closer to selling to new owners as stiff competition, rising commodity costs and waning consumer demand are undercutting ​performance, according to five people familiar with the discussions.

item.title: Fed's Hammack: Rates likely on hold 'for a good while' - CNBC
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said on Wednesday that while she sees no imminent need for the ‌central bank to change its interest rate target setting, it’s possible cuts or even hikes could lie ahead.

item.title: UK financial watchdog to consult on proposed crypto regulations
item.description: Britain's financial regulator said on ​Wednesday it was ‌consulting on how firms might be ​affected by ​the regulation of the ⁠crypto industry which ​is due to ​come into effect by October 2027.

item.title: American Eagle rallies again after launching second Sydney Sweeney campaign
item.description: American Eagle Outfitters shares jumped on Wednesday after the denim retailer unveiled a second campaign with "Euphoria" star Sydney Sweeney, ​a year after a viral and controversial ad featuring ‌the actress fueled a stock rally.

item.title: Adobe releases AI assistant for creative tools, says it will work with Anthropic's Claude
item.description: Adobe said on Wednesday it was releasing a new artificial intelligence assistant designed to help users carry out tasks ​across its suite of software for editing photos, videos and other ‌digital content.

item.title: IMF cautions countries against broad fuel subsidies to deal with war-driven energy shock
item.description: The war in the Middle East has intensified strains on an already fragile global fiscal situation, with higher interest rates and rising energy prices already fueling calls for support from emerging markets and developing economies, the International Monetary Fund said on Wednesday in its ​Fiscal Monitor report.

item.title: Jane Street signs $6 billion AI cloud deal with CoreWeave, boosts stake
item.description: Trading firm Jane Street has committed about $6 billion for CoreWeave's cloud services, marking the third major deal for the ​Nvidia-backed neocloud company in a week and underscoring surging demand ‌for computing capacity.

item.title: US import prices increase below expectations; sharp rise anticipated due to Iran war
item.description: U.S. import prices increased less than expected in March, though the trend still pointed to firming imported inflationary pressures as the Middle East conflict boosts oil prices and snarls supply chains.

item.title: Economic growth will be slower this quarter amid war, US Treasury chief says
item.description: U.S. Treasury ​Secretary Scott Bessent said on ‌Wednesday that the economy will be slower this quarter amid the ​Iran war, but it ​is in good shape and ⁠will rebound, adding that ​oil prices do not appear ​to be weighing on inflation expectations.

item.title: China's CATL beats first quarter forecasts, boosting market dominance
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle battery giant CATL posted first-quarter results that surpassed expectations on Wednesday, strengthening its market ​dominance even as EV demand softens.

item.title: Silver faces sixth year of deficit with stock drawdown raising squeeze risks, research shows
item.description: The silver market is heading for a ‌sixth year of structural deficit, with 762 million troy ounces drawn from stocks since 2021, raising the risk of a renewed liquidity squeeze despite weaker demand expectations, the Silver Institute and consultancy Metals Focus said on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump backs government AI safeguards in banking system, acknowledges risks
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump acknowledged the risks ​artificial intelligence posed to the banking system ‌and said there should be government safeguards, but also believed the technology could make the ​banking system better and safer, according ​to an interview broadcast on Wednesday.

item.title: US Treasury asks private credit firms for data, Punchbowl News reports
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department ​has begun asking ‌private credit firms to submit information ​about their ​business models and ties ⁠to the ​regulated financial system, ​Punchbowl News reported on Wednesday citing sources ​familiar with ​the matter.

item.title: Nutella-maker Ferrero aware of EU inspections, says it is cooperating
item.description: Nutella-maker Ferrero said on Wednesday ​that the company is ‌aware that on-site inspections are currently taking place ​in its offices ​by European Commission officials.
